content: Just as 2025's configurable Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) rewards AI-powered autonomous agents for exploring novel uncharted environments to drive self-improving task performance, biological agents (from microbes to wildlife) can be framed as naturally evolved intrinsic curiosity-driven agents that seek out novel biochemical and ecological stimuli to improve their long-term adaptive fitness. This framework opens a new interdisciplinary approach to modeling microbial colonization and ecosystem niche exploration using cutting-edge ICM-based reinforcement learning tools.
